mission.title: Install and Configure Spire on Kubernetes
mission.description: SPIRE (the SPIFFE Runtime Environment) provides a set of APIs and services to manage identities in a cloud-native environment. Installing SPIRE allows you to secure your workloads by providing them with strong identities and enabling secure communication between them.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the SPIRE Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SPIRE using Helm
steps.description:
Install SPIRE in the `spire` namespace with the specified version.
```bash
helm install spire spire/spire --namespace spire --create-namespace --version 0.28.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the SPIRE pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spire
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the SPIRE server and agent. Edit the Helm release to set resource limits.
```bash
helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire --set server.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set server.resources.limits.memory=512Mi --set agent.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set agent.resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the SPIRE release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall spire --namespace spire
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions associated with SPIRE.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep spire | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the `spire` namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace spire
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace spire -o yaml > spire-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade SPIRE
upgrade.description:
Upgrade SPIRE to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire --version 0.28.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the SPIRE pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may occur due to insufficient resources or node constraints. Check the events for the pod.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods crashing or in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the crashing pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and the pods are running. Check the service endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace spire
kubectl get endpoints <service-name> --namespace spire
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SPIRE will have all pods running in the `spire` namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring they are in the Running state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add spire https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened
helm repo update

helm upgrade spire spire/spire --namespace spire --set server.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set server.resources.limits.memory=512Mi --set agent.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set agent.resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
